task_updates.executor_notes: Added test_rendered_startup_script_auto_heals_lockfile_drift using startup_script.render_startup_script. The test asserts set +e / locked sync / SYNC_RC / set -e ordering, the exact drift grep, warning marker, uv lock, unlocked recovery uv sync, post-warning UV_LOCK_SHA256 recompute, auto-heal sentinel write ordering via script.rindex(...), auto-heal success marker, and non-drift abort marker. The existing sentinel-skip test was left unchanged and the full startup-script test file passes. First full-file run failed because the new test looked for set +e after the locked sync; corrected the assertion to the actual intended order set +e < locked sync < SYNC_RC < set -e, then reran the full file successfully.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. The new test uses script.rindex(...) for the auto-heal sentinel-write ordering check, asserts set +e/SYNC_RC=$?/set -e around the captured sync, the exact drift grep, warning marker, uv lock, unlocked recovery sync, post-warning sha256 recompute, auto-heal success line, and non-drift abort line. The existing sentinel-skip test was not modified and passed in the full test-file run.
